issues_found: Source is over 10 years old (published November 2015) - significant temporal gap from TODAY (2026-04-10)

event_type is 'research_paper' but source is more accurately described as an economic commentary/blog post; source reads like a news-style summary of quarterly data rather than a formal research paper
Topic classification as 'market structure' is borderline - this is primarily household credit/consumer finance data; market_structure_context field acknowledges this tension
market_impact.confidence is set to 0 with empty asset_classes - appropriate given the source contains no market impact language, but this signals limited utility for trading/alpha generation
human_review_reason: Source is authoritative (NY Fed) and record is factually accurate, but the 10-year temporal gap significantly limits relevance for current market analysis. The record correctly notes this in quality_tier (tier C, score 5). Decision needed on whether historical quarterly credit data from 2015 has sufficient archival value to justify inclusion, or if the age makes it too stale despite accuracy.
